title: A Distributed Gaussian Process Model for Multi-Robot Mapping

proposed_solution.name: DistGP
proposed_solution.description: A multi-robot algorithm to fit an arbitrary global function using only local observation and computation with peer-to-peer communication
proposed_solution.key_technique: Sparse GPs with inducing points as local summaries

methodology.improvement: Loopy model that relaxes tree constraint to allow cycles

results.accuracy: Loopy model shows substantial improvement in accuracy over TSGPs
results.adaptability: Better suited to dynamic and asynchronous settings

contributions: New distributed GP model trainable with GBP, outperforming TSGPs
Empirical analysis showing model can be built on-the-fly with distributed, asynchronous inference
Experimental evaluation on environmental monitoring and occupancy mapping, showing superior performance to DiNNO

related_work.most_similar: DiNNO: A distributed NN optimizer for multi-robot learning
other_approaches: Distributed neural maps using consensus ADMM
Distributed neural maps using direct regularization
